heading: ReMemory
summary_1: ReMemory protects your files by splitting a key among people you trust. You decide how many must come together to unlock them — three of five friends, two of two partners, whatever fits. No single person can access anything alone. Based on Shamir's Secret Sharing.
summary_2: Each person gets a bundle with everything they need to recover. It works in any browser, offline*, with no accounts or servers. If this website disappears, recovery still works.
how_subtitle: Your files are encrypted. The key is split into pieces. When enough people bring their pieces together, the files are unlocked.
how_caption: Any three of five can recover the files
how_fallback:
Your File → Encrypt → Split key into 5 pieces → Distribute to friends
↓
Any 3 friends → Combine pieces → Unlock → File recovered
whatis_is_1: A tool that runs in your browser, offline*
whatis_is_2: A way to split a secret key among people you trust
whatis_is_4: Self-contained — recovery works without this website
whatis_isnt_2: An account system or cloud product
whatis_isnt_3: Something that stores your data anywhere
try_step_3: Drag Bob's and Camila's README files onto the page
try_step_4: When enough pieces are added, the files unlock
try_note: This is the closest thing to what a real recovery feels like.
trust_1: Recovery works without this project existing — each bundle is self-contained
trust_2: The code is open source — read it on GitHub
trust_3: A security review explains the cryptographic choices
trust_4: Everything runs locally. Your files never leave your device
trust_5: Try the demo bundles before using it with anything important
trust_6: Encryption uses age — a modern, well-regarded tool
trust_7: Read more about what protects your data and what could go wrong
bg_1: Two things led me to build ReMemory.
bg_2: First, I watched a documentary about Clive Wearing, a man who has lived with severe amnesia since 1985. Seeing how fragile memory can be made me think about what would happen to my digital life if something similar happened to me.
bg_3: Second, I've had several concussions from cycling accidents. Each time, I've been lucky to recover fully — but the reminders add up.
bg_4: ReMemory came out of that: a way to ensure the people I trust can access what matters, even if I can't help them.
bg_changelog: ReMemory keeps growing. Check the changelog to see what's new.
footer_timelock: * Time-locked archives need a brief internet connection at recovery time.
